---
title: "RP 17: Self-Care as Relapse Prevention"
format: html
---
# RP 17: Self-Care as Relapse Prevention

Self-care is not indulgence; it is risk management. Sleep loss, poor nutrition, inactivity, and chronic stress increase emotional reactivity and cravings. When baseline health drops, relapse risk rises.

A strong self-care plan includes basic body care, emotional care, and practical care. The most useful plans are simple enough to repeat even on hard days.

Self-care practices improve relapse resistance by stabilizing sleep, stress response, and emotional regulation. These baseline factors strongly influence craving intensity and decision quality under pressure.

## Self-Care Baseline Checklist
- [ ] I get enough sleep most nights.
- [ ] I eat regular meals.
- [ ] I hydrate throughout the day.
- [ ] I move my body most days.
- [ ] I have at least one stress-down skill.
- [ ] I ask for support before crisis.

::: {.callout-caution}
### When risk is higher

If urges or stress rise quickly, use this sequence early rather than waiting.

Choose the two boxes you left unchecked and turn them into this week's goals.

Early action usually prevents the cycle from becoming harder to interrupt.
:::
